thinking: From a standing first-person view in a cafe-like kitchen/dining area, I face a yellow table with chairs; a female customer sits at the near side, leaning slightly forward with her arms near the tabletop, while cabinets, pots, a kettle, sink, refrigerator, and range hood line the back wall. The customer shifts posture, places both hands on the table, and rises from the chair, turning toward me and stepping left past the table edge. As she approaches very close to the camera and exits out of frame on the left, I pivot and move slightly right/backward, opening the view toward the empty floor, partition wall, and back counter area. After she leaves, I continue scanning/panning around the room, with the table and chairs remaining on the left and the kitchen fixtures visible behind them.
caption: CLERK: Intent to monitor or make space for the customer, achieved by pausing near the table and then turning/shifting aside as she stood and passed. CUSTOMER: Intent to leave the table area or approach service, expressed by pushing up with her hands, standing from the chair, and walking past the camera toward the left.

thinking: I face a sparse cafe/kitchen area with a yellow table and chairs on the left, cabinets, sink, stove hood, pots, and a refrigerator along the back wall; a woman customer sits at the table. The customer leans forward, places weight on the table/chair, and rises from her seat, moving toward the camera and then out of frame to the left. As clerk, I shift my view and walk/pan rightward away from the table, leaving the customer behind and orienting toward the open floor and the partition near the kitchen fixtures. The final view centers more on the partition wall, refrigerator, sink, and counter area, with the table and chairs partly cropped at the left edge.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to leave the seated customer area and move toward the back/kitchen side, achieved by turning right and walking toward the partition and appliances. CUSTOMER: Intent appeared to be to get up and approach or depart from the table, expressed by leaning forward, pushing up from the chair/table, standing, and moving left past the camera.

thinking: I stand in a kitchen-like cafe area facing a yellow table where a woman customer sits on a wooden chair near the left side of the room; cabinets, pots, a sink, a range hood, and a refrigerator are visible behind her. The customer leans forward over the table, places her hands near the tabletop/chair, and pushes herself up from sitting to standing while turning slightly toward me. I then move/pan rightward and forward away from the table, causing the customer to pass out of frame on the left as the blank wall corridor and the partition become central. The scene ends with me oriented toward the partition and kitchen fixtures, no other customer actions visible.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to leave the seated customer area or reposition within the cafe, and I did so by turning and walking rightward toward the partition/kitchen side. CUSTOMER: Intent appeared to be to get up and engage or prepare to move, expressed by leaning forward, bracing on the table/chair, standing up, and orienting toward the clerk.

thinking: I face a yellow dining table in a sparse cafe/kitchen area; a female customer sits on the near-right chair beside the table, with cabinets, sink, stove pots, and a refrigerator visible along the left wall. The customer leans forward, places her hands on the tabletop, and pushes herself up from the chair, then turns toward me and steps around the table edge. I remain standing and then move/pan rightward away from the table, while the customer comes close to the camera on the left edge before leaving view. The camera continues toward the back-right partition/corridor area, with the table and chair sliding out of frame and the kitchen fixtures becoming partially occluded by the wall.
caption: CLERK: Intent-Action Pair: to leave or reposition toward the back area, I turned right and walked away from the table toward the partitioned corridor. CUSTOMER: Intent-Action Pair: to approach or engage the clerk, she stood up from her chair using the table for support and walked toward the camera from the table.

thinking: I face a small dining/kitchen area with a yellow table and chairs in the foreground, cabinets, stove hood, pots, kettle, sink, and refrigerator along the back wall; a woman customer sits at the near-right side of the table. She leans forward over the tabletop, then pushes herself up from the chair and steps toward me, looking in my direction as she comes around the table edge. I remain mostly stationary at first, then turn and move rightward/backward away from the table and customer toward the open floor beside a partition wall. As I continue moving, the customer exits out of the left edge of view while the frame centers on the partition, kitchen counter, and refrigerator.
caption: CLERK: My apparent objective was to leave or reposition away from the table area, and I did so by turning and walking toward the open space beside the partition. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want attention or to approach/interact with me, and she expressed it by rising from her chair, leaning on the table for support, and walking toward my position.

thinking: I face a yellow dining table in a cafe-like room with wooden flooring, chairs, a counter with pots and kettle, a sink, a refrigerator, and a large partition wall. A female customer seated at the far side of the table leans forward, pushes up from the chair, and rises to standing beside the table while looking toward me. I then move to the right, passing close by her and the chair, and continue walking toward the open space and partition, shifting my view away from the customer. The customer remains near the table as I head toward the back/prep area.
caption: CLERK: Intent to leave the customer area and go toward the back/prep side; action: turned right and walked past the table toward the partition and kitchen fixtures. CUSTOMER: Intent to get attention or prepare to engage/leave; action: leaned forward, stood up from the chair, and faced toward the clerk.

thinking: From a stationary first-person view in a cafe-like room, a female customer sits at the far side of a rectangular wooden table near the kitchenette; chairs surround the table, and cabinets, pots, a sink, range hood, and refrigerator are visible behind her. The customer leans forward over the table, places her hands on it, and rises from the chair while turning her face toward the clerk/camera. She steps closer along the table edge, coming near the left side of the frame, while the clerk begins turning and moving rightward away from the table. The camera continues panning/walking toward the open floor and partition wall, leaving the customer and table mostly out of view and bringing the refrigerator and counter area into clearer view.
caption: CLERK: Intent-action pair: to disengage from the table/customer area and move toward the back/workspace, I turned right and walked away toward the partition and kitchen side. CUSTOMER: Intent-action pair: to get the clerk’s attention or approach for service, she pushed off the table, stood up from her chair, faced the clerk, and stepped closer along the table.

thinking: I move down a narrow room with white walls and wood flooring toward a woman customer standing against the right wall; she faces me and briefly raises one hand in a small wave or signal. I continue past her and pan left to reveal a yellow table with matching chairs, a counter with sink and cookware, and a refrigerator. The customer walks from the wall to the near side of the table, places her hands on the tabletop, pulls in close, and sits on the chair. After she settles, a small item appears on the table in front of her, and I remain standing nearby with my hands visible together as if waiting or acknowledging her.
caption: CLERK: My apparent goal was to guide or monitor the customer into the seating area, which I did by approaching her, moving toward the table area, and then stopping nearby with hands together as if ready to assist. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want service or a place to sit, shown by signaling with a raised hand, following toward the table, sitting down, and placing/keeping a small item on the table in front of her.

thinking: I move down a long open room with wood flooring and white walls toward a woman standing against the right wall; she stays mostly still, facing slightly left, with one brief hand movement near her face. As I get closer, I pass her and pan left, revealing a yellow rectangular table with matching chairs, plus a kitchenette area with lower cabinets, sink, kettle, pots, and a tall refrigerator. The woman leaves the wall and walks toward the table, placing her hands on the tabletop and chair as she positions herself. She then lowers into the chair and sits at the far side of the table while I stop nearby facing her.
caption: CLERK: My apparent objective was to approach and monitor/assist the customer area, and I did this by walking toward the woman, then moving to the table area and stopping near her after she sat down. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want a place to sit or wait, expressing this by standing aside initially, then walking to the table, steadying herself with her hands, pulling into position, and sitting in the chair.

thinking: I move forward through a sparse cafe-like room with white walls and wood flooring, initially facing a blank wall and then revealing a woman standing against the right wall. She remains near the wall, then turns and walks with me toward a yellow rectangular table with matching chairs, beside a counter with sink, kettle, cabinets, and a tall refrigerator. The customer reaches the near-right chair, places her hands on the tabletop/chair area, and lowers herself into the seat. I stop near the table, facing her after she sits.
caption: CLERK: Intent to guide or monitor a seated customer; I walked through the room toward the dining area and stopped by the table as she took a seat. CUSTOMER: Intent to find a place to sit or wait; she stood by the wall, followed toward the table, then pulled in close and sat down at the chair.

thinking: I face a mostly empty room with wood flooring and white walls, where a single female customer stands against the right wall; I move steadily closer while she shifts her legs and keeps her body angled toward me. As I continue forward, the left side of the room comes into view with a yellow rectangular table, matching chairs, blue lower cabinets, a sink, countertop appliances, and a tall light-colored refrigerator. The customer walks from the wall toward the near side of the table, places her hands on the tabletop, bends forward, then lowers herself into the chair on the far/right side of the table. I stop near the table edge facing her, with my hand briefly visible in front, while she settles into a seated waiting posture.
caption: CLERK: My apparent objective was to guide or monitor the customer to a seating area, and I achieved it by walking toward her and accompanying her to the table. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want a place to sit or wait, expressing this by approaching the table, bracing on it with her hands, and then sitting down in the chair.

thinking: I move through a sparse cafe-like room with wood flooring and white walls, approaching a woman standing against the right wall with her arms crossed. She uncrosses her arms, gestures lightly, then walks with me toward a yellow table with matching chairs near a kitchenette area with cabinets, sink, stove hood, pots, and a refrigerator. The customer steps up to the near-side chair, places her hands on the tabletop, and lowers herself into the chair. I stop across from the table and gesture with my hand toward her and the seating area as she settles in.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to guide and seat the customer, achieved by approaching her, leading her toward the table, and gesturing to the seating area from across the table. CUSTOMER: Intent was to wait for service and then take a seat, shown by standing by the wall, following the clerk to the table, touching the table/chair, and sitting down.

thinking: I move down a narrow room with wood flooring and white walls toward a woman standing along the right wall, while the left side gradually reveals a yellow table, matching chairs, cabinets, sink, pots, and a refrigerator. The customer, a woman in a light green shirt and pink pants, waits by the wall, briefly lifting a hand near her face and then turning toward the table as I approach and gesture with my hand. She walks beside the table, places her hands near the tabletop/chair area, and lowers herself into the chair on the far side of the yellow table. I stop near the table facing her after she is seated, with the kitchen fixtures and chairs visible around us.
caption: CLERK: My apparent goal was to guide or seat the customer, and I approached her, gestured toward the table, and stood nearby as she moved to the chair. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want a place to sit or to follow service direction, and she responded by turning from the wall, walking to the table, pulling in close to the chair/table, and sitting down.

thinking: I move forward through a long, bright cafe room toward a yellow rectangular table on the left where a single seated woman waits quietly. As I get closer and angle left, more of the kitchenette becomes visible behind her: blue lower cabinets, sink, pots, kettle, range hood, and a tall off-white refrigerator. The customer remains seated with a calm posture, occasionally shifting her gaze and keeping one hand near the tabletop as if waiting to be served or acknowledged. Near the end, I step up to the table and extend my hand toward the tabletop, appearing to place or point to a small white item in front of her.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to approach and serve/assist the seated customer, achieved by walking to her table and reaching out to place or indicate a small item on the table. CUSTOMER: Intent appeared to be waiting for service or an item, expressed by staying seated at the table, watching the clerk approach, and keeping attention on the tabletop.

thinking: I move forward down a narrow cafe/kitchen area with white walls and wood flooring, gradually panning left to reveal a yellow rectangular table with several wooden chairs and a seated female customer on the far side. As I approach, more of the back counter becomes visible on the left, including cabinets, a sink, pots, a kettle, a range hood, a tall refrigerator, and a side cabinet. The customer remains seated with a neutral posture, occasionally shifting her head and lifting one hand briefly near her face or chest while looking generally toward the table and then toward me. I stop close to the table, facing her from the near corner as if preparing to engage or take an order.
caption: CLERK: Intent to approach and attend to a seated customer; action was walking through the room and stopping beside the table to face her. CUSTOMER: Intent to wait for or signal service; action was sitting at the table, glancing toward the clerk, and briefly raising a hand as a small acknowledgment/request gesture.

thinking: I face a sparse cafe/break area with a yellow rectangular table and chairs on the left, a seated female customer at the far side of the table, and a kitchenette with cabinets, sink, kettle, pots, and a refrigerator behind her. I slowly move leftward and closer to the table, circling toward the customer while keeping her in view; the customer remains seated, looking down and then toward me. Midway through, she raises a hand to her face/head as if resting her cheek or signaling mild attention, then lowers it while staying seated. By the end, I approach the near edge of the table and extend my hands toward the tabletop, as if preparing to address her or place/take something.
caption: CLERK: Intent was to approach and attend to the seated customer; I walked closer around the table and reached my hands toward the tabletop to begin service or interaction. CUSTOMER: Intent appeared to be waiting for assistance or acknowledgment; she stayed seated at the table, looked toward me, and briefly touched/raised her hand to her face while watching my approach.

thinking: I face a mostly empty cafe room with a yellow rectangular table and chairs on the left, where one female customer sits quietly near the far side of the table; a kitchenette with sink, cabinets, stove, pots, kettle, and refrigerator is visible behind her. I slowly move and pan leftward around the table, bringing the seated customer and kitchen area more fully into view while the customer remains seated and looks generally toward me. Midway through, the customer raises one hand high above shoulder level, appearing to signal for attention or service, then keeps watching me as I continue approaching. I end close to the table edge, angled down toward the tabletop and the customer, with my hand briefly appearing near the table as if preparing to engage or serve her.
caption: CLERK: Intended to approach and attend to the seated customer, and did so by walking/panning closer to her table and orienting toward her. CUSTOMER: Intended to get assistance or place a request, and expressed it by staying seated, looking at the clerk, and raising her hand to signal.

thinking: I move slowly from the open floor area toward a yellow table on the left where a single female customer is seated, while the kitchenette with sink, cabinets, stove, pots, and a refrigerator comes further into view behind her. The customer remains seated in a wooden chair, mostly looking downward with a waiting posture as I approach. Midway through, she raises one arm upward in a signaling gesture, then lowers it as I continue closing the distance to the table. By the end, I am standing near the table edge facing her, with my hand briefly visible above the tabletop as if ready to assist or take her request.
caption: CLERK: My intent was to attend to the seated customer, and I walked across the room to her table and positioned myself close to engage. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want service or attention, and she expressed it by sitting and waiting, then raising her hand to signal the clerk.

thinking: I move slowly leftward and closer toward a yellow table in a sparse cafe/kitchen area, bringing a seated female customer into clearer view; behind her are a sink, blue lower cabinets, a refrigerator, and cookware on the counter. The customer remains seated on the far side of the table, mostly looking downward or ahead with a calm posture while I approach. Midway through, she raises one arm high above the table as if signaling for attention, then lowers it as I continue to come nearer. Near the end, my hand appears above the tabletop, extended toward her, suggesting I am about to engage, indicate something, or take her order.
caption: CLERK: Intent to attend to the seated customer; I walked closer to her table and extended my hand over the tabletop to begin interaction or service. CUSTOMER: Intent to get assistance or place a request; she stayed seated and raised her hand to signal for attention, then faced me as I approached.

thinking: I move down a narrow cafe area toward a yellow table on the left where a seated woman customer is visible; the space includes wooden flooring, white walls, yellow chairs, and a back counter with cabinets, sink, pots, kettle, and a refrigerator. As I approach and angle left, the customer looks toward me and raises one hand near her head, appearing to signal for attention or greet me. I continue closing the distance to the table, looking down at her and the tabletop as she lowers her hand and rests an arm on the table. Near the end, I stop beside the table and extend my hands forward over the table, as if preparing to address her order or interact with the table setting.
caption: CLERK: I intended to attend to the seated customer, so I walked over to her table and reached my hands toward the tabletop to begin service. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want the clerk's attention or assistance, which she expressed by looking at me and raising her hand while remaining seated at the table.

thinking: I move forward down a narrow cafe aisle toward a seated female customer at a yellow table on the left; the room has wood flooring, white walls, yellow chairs, and a back counter with cabinets, sink, pots, and a refrigerator. I continue approaching the table until I am standing beside it, facing her more directly. Near the end, my hand comes into view over the table, suggesting I am about to engage with her or respond to her request.

CUSTOMER: Intent to get service or communicate a request; she stayed seated, looked at me, and repeatedly raised and extended her arm to attract attention and indicate her need.

thinking: I move down a narrow cafe area toward a yellow table where a single seated female customer is on the left side of view; the room has wood flooring, white walls, several yellow chairs, and a back counter with cabinets, sink, pots, kettle, and a tall refrigerator. Near the end, I step up to the table edge and extend my hand over the tabletop as if to address her, point something out, or prepare to serve/clear the table.

CUSTOMER: Intent to wait for service or engage the clerk while seated; action: stayed seated facing the table/clerk and briefly touched her head before returning her hand down.

thinking: I walk down a narrow cafe/kitchen area toward a yellow rectangular table on the left, where a seated female customer is visible beside several wooden chairs; cabinets, a sink, cookware, and a refrigerator come into view along the back wall as I get closer. The customer remains seated at the far side of the table, initially looking down with one hand near the tabletop, then briefly raises a hand to her head and looks toward me as I approach. I continue moving to the near edge of the table, angling my view downward toward her place setting area. At the end, my hand extends a small flat white item over the table toward the customer, as if presenting a receipt, menu, or bill.

CUSTOMER: Intent to receive service or respond to the clerk; she stayed seated, looked up from the table, touched her head briefly, and oriented her attention toward me as I arrived.

thinking: I move down a narrow room toward a yellow table on the left where a single female customer is seated; the background reveals a kitchenette with blue lower cabinets, a sink, pots, and a tall refrigerator. As I approach and angle left around the table, the customer remains seated facing me, briefly raising a hand near her face and then resting with her hands lower, watching quietly. I continue closing the distance to the near edge of the table, bringing the tabletop and empty chairs into the foreground. At the end, my hands come forward over the table as if preparing to place, present, or discuss something with the seated customer.

CUSTOMER: Intent to receive attention/service while waiting; she stayed seated at the table, looked toward me, and made a small hand-to-face gesture before remaining still.

thinking: I move down a narrow cafe/kitchen area toward a yellow rectangular table on the left where one female customer is seated alone on the far side. As I approach, more of the kitchen becomes visible behind her: blue lower cabinets, sink, countertop appliances, stove pots, and a tall cream refrigerator. The customer first raises a hand to her forehead/face, then lowers it and sits facing me quietly with her hands near her lap or the table edge. Near the end, I stop beside the table and extend my hands forward over the tabletop toward her, as if greeting her, presenting something, or preparing to take her order.
caption: CLERK: I appeared to approach the seated customer to attend to her table, and I did this by walking up beside the yellow table and reaching my hands out over it. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want service or attention while seated, expressing this by remaining at the table, looking toward me, and briefly touching her face before waiting calmly.

thinking: I move forward down a narrow cafe aisle and angle left toward a yellow table where a seated female customer is on the left side of view. As I get closer, more of the cafe setup appears on the left: yellow chairs, a counter with sink and cabinets, pots, and a refrigerator. The customer looks toward me and raises one arm high, holding it up as if signaling for service or trying to get attention. I continue approaching the table until I am standing close beside her, and she then lowers her hand and sits waiting with her hands near her lap/table edge.
caption: CLERK: I appeared to be responding to a seated customer's request, walking across the cafe toward her table to assist. CUSTOMER: She appeared to want service or attention, expressing it by looking at me and raising her hand while remaining seated at the table.
